## Purpose
This file outlines a refactor plan for the DiffDoc tool, aiming to enhance its scalability across large codebases by implementing a new architecture for generating and storing summaries as independent JSON files.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users will interact with the DiffDoc tool through CLI commands such as `summarize` and `embed`, which will generate summaries, manage manifests, and handle file changes while providing feedback on processing results, including any errors encountered.

## Business Rules
The plan includes several key decisions: it allows for a breaking schema change without a migration path, mandates atomic writes for file operations, and specifies that orphan summaries should be cleaned up immediately. It also establishes rules for filtering files and handling errors during processing.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Data inputs include source files to be summarized, command-line arguments for configuration, and the existing manifest. Outputs consist of JSON summary files stored under `.diffdoc/summaries/`, a manifest file, and any error reports generated during processing.

## Side Effects
The refactor will result in the creation of new JSON files for summaries and a manifest, as well as potential deletions of orphaned summary files when their corresponding source files are no longer referenced.

## Error And Edge Cases
The plan addresses error handling by allowing the summarization process to continue despite per-file errors, with a non-zero exit status if any failures occur. It also outlines how to handle file deletions and changes in hash values.

## Dependencies
The plan does not specify any external or internal dependencies, indicating that it is designed to operate independently within the existing DiffDoc framework.

## Operational Notes
The implementation will require careful attention to atomic write operations to ensure data integrity. The plan also highlights the need for a filtering strategy to manage which files are processed, as well as the importance of handling duplicate content and orphan cleanup effectively.
